g.text: Syscall Overhead vs Polling CPU Cost — Finding the Crossover Point

div.p: Syscall cost dominates at high rates At 500K ops/sec:
Fixed polling cost, zero syscall overhead At 500K ops/sec:

g.text: Syscall cost | ~80-150ns | Context switch + kernel entry
Polling cost | ~10-15% CPU | One dedicated core
Below break-even | No SQPOLL | Syscalls cheaper than polling
Above break-even | Use SQPOLL | Syscalls exceed polling cost
Game Server | 5-20 | Bursty traffic, need quick response
Background Worker | 500-1000 | Very low submission rate

ol.li: If thread sleeps too often → increase idle
If CPU waste during quiet periods → decrease idle

g.text: Submission pattern | Continuous | Bursty with gaps

div.p: SQPOLL requires CAP_SYS_NICE or root Without privileges, io_uring_queue_init_params returns -EPERM. Always implement graceful fallback to non-SQPOLL mode.

g.text: fprintf(stderr, "SQPOLL requires CAP_SYS_NICE, using default mode\n");
return ret; // 0 on success, negative errno on failure

text.tspan: SQPOLL requires CAP_SYS_NICE, using default mode
// 0 on success, negative errno on failure
